title: CPI vs XServer Business for Organizations in Japan
metaTitle: CPI vs XServer Business: Pricing, Backup and SLA
description: Compare CPI Business Standard and XServer Business using official pricing, storage, backup, SLA, email, support, and migration information.
lead: CPI emphasizes SmartRelease, separated domain environments, and business email operations. XServer Business emphasizes capacity, fourteen-day remote backups, migration assistance, and administrative controls.
author: Luqevora Editorial Team

sources.label: CPI Business Standard pricing and onboarding

sections.heading: Conclusion: CPI for release workflows, XServer Business for capacity and migration support
body: CPI is a strong candidate when staging, controlled production releases, separated domain environments, and Japanese business email are central requirements.

XServer Business is easier to shortlist when the organization prioritizes a larger shared-server storage allocation, fourteen-day backups that include mail, administrator users, setup assistance, and website migration support.
bullets: Choose CPI when staging and controlled release are central
Choose XServer Business when capacity and migration support are central
For both services, verify the precise SLA scope and paid options

sections.heading: Officially published differences
body: The table compares the published shared hosting configurations at the verification date. Temporary campaign prices should not be treated as permanent pricing.

rows: 300 GB primary plus 100 GB per multi-domain environment
Combined 10 GB web and database, up to 30 generations
Web, mail and MySQL, previous 14 days, remote storage
100 percent reference for the primary web server only

sections.heading: Pricing and total cost
body: CPI displayed JPY 4,840 per month equivalent for a twelve-month contract with no setup fee. XServer Business displayed JPY 4,180 per month for the twelve-month shared Standard plan plus a JPY 16,500 setup fee. A temporary cashback campaign was visible on XServer Business at verification.
Compare renewal pricing, setup fees, migration work, backup requirements, domains, and optional support rather than using one promotional number.

sections.heading: Backup and publishing operations
body: CPI SmartRelease combines staging, production release, and recovery. Its standard backup allowance is a combined 10 GB for web and database data, retained for up to 30 generations.
XServer Business states that web files, mail data, and MySQL databases are kept for the previous 14 days and stored remotely. Confirm recovery granularity and operational procedures before purchase.

sections.heading: Email, administration and support
body: CPI publishes SPF, DKIM, DMARC, webmail, forwarding, and autoresponder support. Standard support is email-based, while the 24-hour telephone service is a paid option.
XServer Business includes telephone, email, and chat support and provides administrator-user controls. Some advanced multi-account and dedicated-IP features depend on the selected plan.

sections.heading: How to choose
body: Document the publishing process, support responsibilities, data recovery objectives, and required administrator roles before selecting a service.
bullets: CPI fit: agency or internal team needs staging and release control
CPI fit: multiple domains and Japanese business email need separated environments
XServer Business fit: large storage, migration support and standard multi-channel support
XServer Business fit: administrator controls and corporate domain benefits are important

faqs.question: Which service had the lower twelve-month monthly price at verification?
faqs.answer: XServer Business shared Standard displayed JPY 4,180 per month, while CPI displayed JPY 4,840 per month equivalent. Setup fees and campaign conditions make total cost different.

faqs.question: Which backup model is broader?
faqs.answer: CPI provides up to 30 generations within a combined 10 GB web and database allowance. XServer Business publishes fourteen days of web, mail and MySQL backups with remote storage. Choose based on data scope and recovery needs.

faqs.question: Which is better for a web agency?
faqs.answer: CPI is attractive for staging and controlled releases through SmartRelease. XServer Business is attractive when migration assistance, capacity, and administrative controls are more important.
